free_text: Cross sections of proton-induced reactions on natGd
with special emphasis on the production possibilities
of 152Tb and 155Tb
Cape Peninsula Univ. of Technology, Bellville
High-purity Gd foils.
Stacked foil
activation technique was used. Three irradiations
were performed, one in iThemba LABS in Faure, South
Africa and two in Chiba, Japan. At iThemba LABS, the
stack consisted of high-purity Gd foils of 25.2
mg/cm2 thickness, 28.9 mg/cm2 thick Cu degrader foils,
high-purity Al foils of 3.24 mg/cm2 thickness and Ti
foils of 11.5 mg/cm2 thickness were used as monitor
foils. The stack was bombarded in an accurately
calibrated Faraday chamber mounted at an external
beam line. The beam was 4 mm in diameter. The average
beam current was 50 nA and the irradiation time was 3
h. The irradiation was done in vacuum with a beam
energy of nominally 66.0 MeV. At NIRS, the first
stack consisted of 17 high-purity Gd foils of 18.49
mg/cm2 thickness and Co 235.85 and 239.41 mg/cm2 and
Au 100.69 mg/cm2 degrader foils. The Co foils were
used above 32 MeV and Au below 32 MeV. Ti foils of
4.58 mg/cm2 thickness were used as monitor foils. The
second stack consisted of 9 high-purity Gd foils of
which the first five were 18.49 mg/cm2 thick and the
next four were 6.8 mg/cm2 thick. Only Au degrader of
100.69 mg/cm2 were used in this stack. Ti monitor
foils of 14.08 mg/cm2 were used. Both stacks were
irradiated with beam of 4 mm in diameter at beam
energies of 70.2 and 30.1 MeV. Due to the window foil
the nominal incident energies on the stacks had
slightly degraded values of 66.7 and 29.4 MeV. The
first irradiation lasted for 1.5 h at an average beam
current of 50 nA. The second irradiation lasted for
3.8 h with an average beam current of 70 nA.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were estimated
taking the independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing components (12-20%).
The uncertainty in energy of each measured
data point was estimated from the uncertainty in
incident beam energy, foil thickness and energy
straggling in the stack.
The total uncertainties in the measured
cross sections were obtained by summing all the
contributing uncertainties in quadrature and are
expressed with a 1sigma (68%) confidence level.
statistical and peak fitting (1-7%)
beam current (3%)
detector efficiency (5%)
counting geometry (1%)
effective target thickness (3%)
decay corrections (2%)
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
